unit: units (Passenger Vehicles wholesale — OEM to dealer)
data_notes: July 2025 monthly figures DERIVED from Q2 FY26 cumulative (Apr-Sep 2025) minus confirmed Aug and Sep monthly values. Derivation noted per entry. SIAM data represents wholesale dispatches from OEM to dealer — not retail sales to end consumers. Retail (VAHAN registration) data tracks differently. Apr 2025 two-wheeler figure is below prior year due to high base effect from pre-buying ahead of OBD2B emission norms that kicked in Apr 2025. Q4 FY26 data for March 2026 not yet released as of April 5, 2026.

series.source: SIAM Monthly Flash Sales, April 2025. PV 3,48,847 (+3.9% YoY from 3,35,746), 2W 14,58,784 (-16.7% YoY from 17,51,419 — high base due to pre-buying ahead of OBD2B norm). 3W 42,441 (Q1 total 1,65,211 minus May 53,942 and Jun 68,828 = derived). Business Standard citing SIAM official press release.
SIAM Monthly Flash Sales, May 2025. PV 3,44,656 (-0.8% YoY), 2W 16,55,927 (+2.2% YoY), 3W 53,942 (-3.3% YoY). Business Standard and Economic Times citing SIAM official press release dated June 2025.
SIAM Monthly Flash Sales, June 2025. PV 3,12,849, 2W 15,59,851, 3W 68,828. Q1 FY26 total: PV 10,06,352, 2W 46,74,562, 3W 1,65,211. Business Standard citing SIAM official Q1 FY26 press release.

DERIVED: Q2 FY26 total (Apr-Sep 2025) minus Q1 FY26 total (Apr-Jun 2025) minus Aug-25 and Sep-25 confirmed monthly figures. PV: Q2 total approx 20,24,749 minus Q1 10,06,352 = Apr-Sep second half 10,18,397; minus Aug 3,21,840 minus Sep 3,72,458 = Jul 3,24,099. Note: derivation approximate — Q2 total sourced from SIAM H1 FY26 press release. Figures are indicative; exact July data requires direct SIAM press release verification.
SIAM Monthly Flash Sales, August 2025. PV 3,21,840, 2W 18,33,921, 3W 75,759. Business Standard citing SIAM official press release dated September 2025.

SIAM Monthly Flash Sales, September 2025. PV 3,72,458 (+4.4% YoY), 2W 21,60,889 (highest ever September), 3W 84,077. H1 FY26 (Apr-Sep 2025) total: PV 20,24,652, 2W 1,12,27,639. Business Standard and Autocar India citing SIAM official H1 FY26 press release.

SIAM Monthly Flash Sales, October 2025. PV 4,60,739, 2W 22,10,727, 3W 81,288. Festive season (Navratri, Dussehra, Dhanteras, Diwali) drove record monthly volumes in PV and 2W segments. Business Standard citing SIAM official press release dated November 2025.

SIAM Monthly Flash Sales, November 2025. PV 4,12,405, 2W 19,44,475, 3W 71,999. Post-festive month — sequential decline from October peaks as expected. Economic Times citing SIAM official press release dated December 2025.

SIAM Monthly Flash Sales, December 2025. PV 3,99,216 (+26.8% YoY), 2W 15,41,036 (+39% YoY), 3W 61,924. Q3 FY26 (Oct-Dec 2025) highest ever quarterly sales for both PV and 2W segments. Business Standard citing SIAM official Q3 FY26 press release.

SIAM Monthly Flash Sales, January 2026. PV 4,49,616 (+12.6% YoY — all-time high January), 2W 19,25,603 (+26.2% YoY — all-time high January), 3W 75,725 (+30.2% YoY). Strong consumer demand momentum and urban income recovery driving volumes. Business Standard citing SIAM official press release dated February 2026.

SIAM Monthly Flash Sales, February 2026. PV 4,17,705 (+10.6% YoY), 2W 18,71,406 (+35.2% YoY), 3W 74,573. Sequential decline from January's record is typical for February (fewer working days). Business Standard citing SIAM official press release dated March 2026.

Not yet released as of April 5, 2026. March 2026 SIAM Flash expected mid-April 2026. ACTION: Populate from siam.in/publications.aspx when released. March is typically the highest month of Q4 with year-end push by OEMs and dealers.

notes: FY26 auto sales show strong recovery after a mixed FY25. H1 FY26 (Apr-Sep) was muted as high base effects from pre-OBD2B buying in FY25 suppressed 2W YoY comparisons. H2 FY26 dramatically stronger: festive season (Oct 2025) saw record 2W and PV volumes; Q3 FY26 (Oct-Dec 2025) was the highest ever quarter for both PV (+26.8% in Dec) and 2W (+39% in Dec). January 2026 set all-time high for both segments. PV segment particularly strong driven by SUV demand (>50% of PV mix), upgrading from hatchbacks, and new launches. 2W recovery driven by rural income recovery (good monsoon + MSP hikes) and finance availability improvement post-RBI rate cuts.
